programs.name: Extended Hours: PM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care after the end of the camp day for children registered in City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp. Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your families balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.  Each individual must register for this activity.

programs.name: Workshop: Weaving
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? This workshop is for weaving enthusiasts of all levels. Here, you’ll explore the art of weaving through guided, hands-on instruction, learning foundational techniques while creating your own unique pieces. Whether you're new to weaving or looking to refine your skills, this class offers a relaxed and creative space to slow down, connect with the process, and bring your ideas to life. One thread at a time!

programs.name: CampTO Explore
programs.description: CampTO Explore is a fun and creative all-day experience. Enjoy games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. Water play or swim days will also be enjoyed, dependent on weather and camp location. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Want to liven up the kitchen table? Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Gymnastics - Intermediate
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on improving strength and form, you will learn and practice skills such as front and back rolls, handstands, cartwheels, bridges, leaps, jumps and turns on a variety of apparatuses. programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). Learn comprehensive first aid training covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the proper use of automated external defibrillators.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED - Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). This recertification course provides a review of first aid and CPR skills. Practical skills are assessed through scenarios and knowledge will be tested through a written evaluation.Prerequisite: Current Intermediate First Aid (CSA Z1210:24).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Knitting
programs.description: Keep your hands moving with knitting. Learn how to hold and use knitting needles, to cast on and off, knit and purl and following a pattern create your very own project.

programs.name: Mixed Media
programs.description: Create pictures with acrylic paint. Learn how to mix and choose colours, how to use tools to create different textures and add layers of paint to create depth.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada's Food Guide. programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Advanced Explorations in Pottery

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Walking
programs.description: Walk your way to fitness in the swimming pool! This activity is designed for everyone, including injured athletes, looking for a safe and effective walking program in a low to no-impact environment.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Counsellor in Training
programs.description: Becoming a camp counsellor is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. In this camp, you will learn about program planning and working with campers in an interactive environment. You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.

programs.name: Extended Hours: PM

programs.name: Hand Building: Garden Cottage
programs.description: Get creative with clay  as you explore a variety of hand-building techniques to craft your own charming  decorative garden cottage. In this 5-week workshop, you’ll learn how to use  pinch pots, slabs, and coils to bring a unique and expressive little house to  life. Finish it off with beautiful decorative details, and you’ll have a  one-of-a-kind piece that adds character to your home or garden. For all levels.

programs.name: Hand Building: Teapots
programs.description: Create a one-of-a-kind,  hand-built teapot this summer! In this five-week workshop, you’ll learn how to  craft every element – from the body and spout to the lid and handles – using  slab-building techniques. Along the way, you’ll have the chance to get creative  with sculptural details and explore a variety of decorative finishes to bring  your vision to life. Perfect for all levels. Please note that students must supply their own pottery tools.

programs.name: Intro to Poetry
programs.description: Learn to experiment creatively with words, rhythm, and the basic rules of poetry in these introductory workshops. Students will be given the tools to cultivate inspiration, express themselves freely and partake in the cultural art form that is poetry. No prior writing experience required.

programs.name: Karate - Advanced
programs.description: Learn the fundamentals of competition and perform simple sparring skills while demonstrating exemplary etiquette. Participants in this program should be able to perform fundamental martial art skills and martial-art specific skills.

programs.name: Karate - Advanced
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Learn to Sew: The Basics
programs.description: Learn the basics of sewing in a relaxed, beginner-friendly environment. This class is perfect for anyone who has little to no experience with a sewing machine or hand-stitching. You'll be guided step-by-step through essential skills such as threading a machine, understanding different fabrics, reading simple patterns and mastering foundational stitches. You will complete small projects such as tote bags, cushion covers and bowl cozies. Material fee of $10 (cash only) payable to instructor for practice materials. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Advanced
programs.description: It's never too early or too late to learn to skate. Building on beginner skills and with a focus on edge development, you will learn crossovers, slalom skating, two foot turns, parallel stopping and backward circle thrusts.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to demonstrate forward and backward skating, gliding and stopping.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet and hockey or figure skates.

programs.name: Learn To Skate - Beginner
programs.description: It’s never too early or too late to learn to skate. programs.name: Leather Tooling: Bookmark
programs.description: Guided by Indigenous Knowledge Keeper, Mimmigees E'Pit (Jennie Town), these free workshops help participants develop basic knowledge in tooling leather. Using metal stamps of different distinct shapes, you will learn how to cut and carve lines, grooves and patterns to press, stamp and punch into leather. Students will walk away with a leather bookmark that they have designed. Materials included. These workshops are a special offering and are open for registration on May 11.These workdshops are inspired by The Canadian Library micro installation at the Clark Centre for the Arts created as a memorial to Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women, Girls and Children. As part of the workshop, participants will be invited to wrap a hardcover book to include in this living art installation.
programs.time: 10:00 PM - 1:00 AM on the next day
